details.docstrings: 3/3 - All top-level algorithms (dijkstra, bellman_ford, floyd_warshall, kruskal_mst) have clear, structured docstrings describing purpose, parameters, return values (with structure and meaning), and relevant notes/assumptions. Even the internal find/union helpers have brief docstrings. Coverage and clarity are high.
details.complexity_comments: 4/4 - Each major algorithm includes explicit time and space complexity in Big-O notation within its docstring (Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, Floyd-Warshall, Kruskal). Kruskal also notes the use of union-find. This matches the requirement to document complexity for each algorithm.
details.inline_comments: 3/3 - Inline comments are present and targeted: they explain initialization choices, the use of a priority queue and visited set, lazy deletion behavior, the relaxation loop, negative-cycle detection, matrix initialization in Floyd-Warshall, and union-find behavior in Kruskal. The comments focus on non-obvious logic and are neither sparse nor overly verbose.
